overview: This four-color Reanimator deck aims to bypass the normal game state by casting high-impact creatures directly from the graveyard. It leverages cheap instants and sorceries to fetch targets, then uses mana acceleration and reanimation spells to bring them onto the battlefield for lethal damage or overwhelming board presence. The inclusion of Deceit provides a flexible way to copy and cast these threats efficiently.
win_conditions: Casting Overlord of the Balemurk from the graveyard for immediate combat damage
Using Formidable Speaker to generate massive mana and cast multiple threats in a single turn
Copying and casting reanimation targets with Deceit to overwhelm opponents before they can respond

keep_criteria: Hands with a reanimation spell and a target in the deck or graveyard
Hands containing Deceit paired with a cheap instant or sorcery
Hands with at least two mana sources and a way to interact
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking any graveyard interaction or reanimation spells
Hands with only lands and no early-game impact
Hands missing a clear path to casting a signature threat

early_game: Discard or tutor for reanimation targets into the graveyard
Play lands and cheap cantrips to set up Deceit copies
Hold up counterspells to protect key reanimation plays
mid_game: Execute the combo by casting reanimation spells to bring back Overlord of the Balemurk or Formidable Speaker
Use Deceit to duplicate cheap spells and generate additional value or protection
Apply pressure with reanimated creatures while searching for win conditions
late_game: Rely on Formidable Speaker's mana generation to cast multiple threats per turn
Use Deceit to copy game-ending spells or board wipes if needed
Maintain graveyard depth to continuously reanimate answers and threats

key_cards.name: Overlord of the Balemurk
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and combat threat
key_cards.notes: Prioritize casting this from the graveyard early to secure combat damage before opponents can stabilize.

key_cards.name: Formidable Speaker
key_cards.role: Mana acceleration and secondary win condition
key_cards.notes: Use its ability to generate excess mana for casting multiple reanimation spells or copying Deceit targets in a single turn.

key_cards.name: Deceit
key_cards.notes: Copy cheap instants or sorceries to fetch targets, protect reanimation plays, or generate additional value when combined with graveyard synergies.

sequencing_tips: Always ensure you have a counter or removal ready before casting your reanimation spell
Use Deceit to copy graveyard tutors or discard spells to set up your targets efficiently
Hold up mana for protection before committing to casting Formidable Speaker or Overlord of the Balemurk

matchups.advice: Prioritize reanimating large creatures early to outpace their damage output.
Use Deceit to copy protection spells and ensure your reanimation resolves.
Race them by casting Overlord of the Balemurk on turn two or three.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Archetype name and signature cards imply a graveyard-centric combo strategy focused on casting large creatures early.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Derived from signature card effects and standard reanimator archetype mechanics.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Based on standard reanimator sequencing principles and interaction timing.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Inferred from typical Standard meta responses to graveyard combo decks.
